name: Janus WebRTC Gateway API
what_it_does: Open-source WebRTC server/gateway with REST and WebSocket API — enabling developers to build real-time audio/video communication applications (video conferencing, streaming, recording, SIP integration) via a plugin-based architecture, where Janus acts as the WebRTC server-side signaling and media gateway, with plugins for VideoRoom (multiparty conferencing), Streaming, SIP, AudioBridge, and Recording.
use_cases: Building multi-party video conferencing via Janus VideoRoom plugin for custom video chat applications
Streaming live video via Janus Streaming plugin for broadcast applications
Integrating SIP telephony with WebRTC via Janus SIP plugin for hybrid communication
Recording WebRTC sessions via Janus Recording for compliance and replay
Building real-time audio mixing via Janus AudioBridge for podcast and audio applications
not_for: Teams without real-time communication engineering expertise (use Daily.co or Agora for managed WebRTC)
Production deployments requiring commercial SLA without self-managed infrastructure
Simple video calling without need for server-side control (use WebRTC peer-to-peer directly)
best_when: A real-time communication engineering team needs a self-hosted, fully customizable WebRTC media server — where Janus provides SFU/MCU capabilities, SIP integration, and recording via open-source plugins.
avoid_when: You need a managed WebRTC cloud service, lack server-side WebRTC engineering expertise, or want zero-infrastructure video calling.

auth.notes: Admin secret (api_secret) for HTTP/WebSocket API access. Token-based auth (optional token mechanism). No OAuth2. No fine-grained scopes. Both HTTP REST and WebSocket API interfaces. Admin API on separate port (requires admin_secret). JavaScript client SDK (janus.js). No webhooks — event-driven via WebSocket connection.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Open source (GPL v3) — completely free self-hosted
pricing.paid_tiers: No paid tiers; Meetecho offers commercial support and deployment services
pricing.notes: Italian company (Meetecho, Naples). Created by Lorenzo Miniero and Saúl Ibarra Corretgé. GPL v3 license. GitHub: meetecho/janus-gateway (8,000+ stars). C-based (low latency). REST and WebSocket APIs for signaling. Plugin architecture: VideoRoom (SFU), Streaming, SIP, AudioBridge, TextRoom, RecordPlay, NoSIP. Meetecho is the official commercial entity offering support. Self-hosted on Linux. Benchmarks: 1,000+ concurrent WebRTC connections per server. Used by: Jitsi community, various video conferencing startups. Not managed SaaS — requires Linux server and WebRTC expertise.

performance.rate_limits: Self-hosted — no rate limits; hardware dependent

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST + WebSocket API. Admin secret auth. GPL v3 open source. C-based self-hosted server. JavaScript client SDK. No webhooks — WebSocket events. Italian company (Meetecho). Plugin-based architecture. High expertise required for deployment and use.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Session reads idempotent by session ID. Plugin handle reads idempotent. Session creation not idempotent — one session per connection. Plugin operations depend on plugin type.
known_agent_gotchas: WebSocket API is primary for real-time signaling — REST is for admin/management operations
Plugin-based: different plugins (VideoRoom, Streaming, SIP) have different API message formats
Admin secret required for API access — configure in janus.jcfg before starting
C server — requires Linux build or Docker; not a cloud API to call without self-hosting
Session ID and handle ID management: establish session → attach plugin → send plugin messages
WebRTC STUN/TURN configuration required for NAT traversal — Janus alone isn't enough

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS depends on server config (self-hosted). GPL v3 open source — auditable C code. Admin secret for API auth. No OAuth2. User-controlled data residency. WebRTC media traversal: DTLS/SRTP for media encryption. Admin API on separate port (restrict access). Video/audio streams are sensitive personal communications.
